pages.full_text:
groves, bamboo + vineyards.

    At Vernazza we had lunch overlooking an inlet of waves which had cut through a tunnel in the rock. John & Charles were fascinated. We had spaghetti at pesto + icecream or crème caramel. Afterwards we swam in the harbour + caught a train home. It is very convenient to be able to walk back to the van from the Station.

    We had a delicious plum latticed tart for supper. But it was very expensive. Veg + fruit are not easy to get here. The campsite is ugly but friendly. We are not quite high enough up to get a good view of the sunsets.

__Saturday July 24th__

    We rushed to wash clothes + wash up to catch the 10.00 train into Sestri Levante. But at the Station Simon was furious that the train fare was more for a 5 min. journey into SL than it was for a 25 mins to Corniglia, so we didn't go by train. Simon + Thomas found a bus for us to take instead.
    We window shopped + saw lovely jewelry + the boys bought yet more cars. Later in the day Eve bought spaghetti tongs. We sat on a bench + on the pavement to eat delicious pizza which Simon bought.
    After a lovely 3 hour swim we shopped for

pages.full_text:
food on the way back to the campsite.
    Simon cooked supper - tomatoes, lettuce, ham, bread, olives marinated in dill or cumin?, bread, followed by grapes, plums + chestnut profiteroles.
    Supper is being washed up by Peter + dried by John. William + Thomas will clear up after the next meal.

    We showed the boys my favourite hotel, the Villa Balbi 0185-42.941 (fax 0185-482451)

    At the cake shop Charles screamed + I dragged him across the road. The shopowner asked Simon whether he was the boys' father or big brother.
    Simon + I found the campsite playground on a terrace far down the hillside.

__Sunday July 25th__

    We left in a hurry. Other campers were blocking us in with more caravans, + waiting to put up an awning. Simon bumped + cracked a car light.

    We got onto the autostrada + came off at Rapallo to look for a church. Beautiful expensive towered villas, but no church for miles + miles. Finally we found a gilded St Lawrence, where John misbehaved + we left pretty quick.
    A car [illegible] bumped us as we stopped at a pedestrian crossing - her mini light broke. We were on the Via Aurelia, as was our campsite. It was built from Rome around the coast 1830
pages.notes: Text appears to continue beyond the bottom of the page. 'St Lawrence' refers to a church in Rapallo (San Lorenzo). Phone number for Villa Balbi transcribed as written: 0185-42.941. A word is illegible where a car's action is described ('A car [illegible] bumped us'). The year is not stated; Saturday July 24 + Sunday July 25 together narrow possibilities — within 1989–2003 this combination occurs in: 1993, 1999.
